text.title: mlp-planning Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for mlp-planning. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: All core features must score 4+ on all four layers before claiming MLP status. Delight (Layer 4) cannot compensate for gaps in Functional (1), Reliable (2), or Usable (3).
rule.rationale: Delight applied to a broken or confusing product amplifies the frustration — users notice the personality of a product that still crashes or loses their data more acutely than they would on a dull product.

rule.statement: MLP = MVP + delight. Not more features — better experience of existing features. Audit before building; most products reach MLP by polishing the core, not by adding functionality.
rule.rationale: Adding features to a sub-MLP product increases surface area for friction without addressing the root cause: the existing interactions are not good enough to generate retention.

rule.statement: Require at least one delight idea per category from the audit before picking the sprint focus. Agents default to Personality and Celebration; Speed and Anticipation are underrepresented without forcing.
rule.rationale: Speed is the cheapest and most underrated delight lever — performance budgets on the core action produce retention lift with zero UX redesign cost. Forcing coverage prevents category tunnel-vision.

rule.statement: Score each polish task as Pain × Frequency × Visibility. If any factor is hard to estimate, use additive scoring (Pain + Frequency + Visibility) to avoid a single zero zeroing out a real opportunity.
rule.rationale: The multiplicative formula breaks down when any dimension is genuinely uncertain — a low-frequency interaction that causes severe pain still deserves consideration, but a zero in Frequency produces a score of zero multiplicatively.
